title: Mildly Sour Japanese Pork Gyoza
description: A sour and crunchy Japanese dish featuring minced pork, slow-cooked to perfection without onions or garlic.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine ground pork, chopped cabbage, grated carrots, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, and grated ginger, and mix well until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
When ready to cook, remove the mixture from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Lay a gyoza wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point, and press the edges together to seal the gyoza.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat a large skillet with 1 inch of water over medium heat and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and add the gyoza to the skillet, being careful not to overcrowd.
Cover the skillet with a lid and steam the gyoza for 10 minutes, or until the wrappers are tender and the filling is cooked through.
Remove the lid and increase the heat to medium-high, and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the bottoms of the gyoza are crispy and golden brown.
